articles.description: Kimi Antonelli takes his second pole position in a row as he beats Mercedes team-mate George Russell in qualifying at the Japanese Grand Prix.
articles.title: Japanese Grand Prix: Kimi Antonelli beats George Russell to Suzuka pole

articles.description: Golf star Tiger Woods has been involved in a car crash in Florida, authorities confirm.
articles.title: Tiger Woods car crash: Golfer involved in Florida collision

articles.description: Jordan Henderson says England players will support Ben White after the Arsenal defender was booed by fans at Wembley in Friday's 1-1 draw with Uruguay.
articles.title: Jordan Henderson: Ben White will have support of England players

articles.description: No-one knew what to expect at football's first official penalty shootout - and no-one wanted to go down in history as the first to miss.
articles.title: Looking back at when football introduced penalty shootouts

articles.description: England captain Ben Stokes is still recovering after surgery on a broken cheekbone and will not play for Durham next week as planned.
articles.title: Ben Stokes: England captain's return delayed by recovery from cheek injury

articles.description: World number two Jannik Sinner reaches a fourth Miami Open final thanks to a straight-sets win over Alexander Zverev.
articles.title: Miami Open: Jannik Sinner beats Alexander Zverev to reach final again

articles.description: Speaking to BBC Sport, Moses Itauma discusses his 13-fight pro career, family sacrifices and why Jermaine Franklin represents his toughest test to date.
articles.title: Itauma vs Franklin: British heavyweight reflects on pro career & upcoming bout

articles.description: After another subdued performance in an England shirt, has Phil Foden wasted his last chance to make their World Cup squad?
articles.title: England 1-1 Uruguay: Phil Foden among those who failed to take chance

articles.description: Charly Musonda could have been a household name but his story is one of frustration and unfulfilled potential.
articles.title: What happened to former Chelsea wonderkid Charly Musonda?

articles.description: Japan's Kaori Sakamoto says she "can say goodbye with this performance" after winning world gold in her final competition.
articles.title: World Figure Skating Championships: Kaori Sakamoto wins fourth title in final competition
